title: carex caryophyllea f. refracta
intro: Carex caryophyllea f. refracta, also known as the fragrant sedge, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae and is native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Carex caryophyllea f. refracta include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and protect against infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically to treat various health issues.
